summary: NextBestDirectionSection feature testing complete. All 7 core tests passed: component renders with history, RTL layout, Hebrew header, SVG compass, action suggestion, insight text, and reason footer. The recommendation algorithm correctly analyzes history value_tags to provide behavioral direction guidance.

issues: SVG polyline uses percentage values which causes console error 'Expected number'. Non-blocking but should be fixed by converting to viewBox-relative pixel coordinates.

action_items: FIX (minor): DecisionMap.js line 86 - polyline points should use numeric coordinates, not percentages. Change from `${p.x}%,${p.y}%` to calculated pixel values based on SVG viewBox.
critical_code_review_comments: NextBestDirectionSection.js - Correctly implements recommendation algorithm with 6 priority levels: negative drift, collective gap, replay blind spots, positive momentum, replay preferences, balance deficit
RTL layout properly implemented with dir='rtl' attribute
data-testid attributes present for all critical elements (next-best-direction-section, direction-compass, action-suggestion, direction-insight)
fetchCollectiveLayer integration working for collective gap comparison
Recommendation changes require backend to classify decisions with avoidance/harm value_tags - the frontend logic is correct, it depends on backend classification
seed_data_creation: Created test decisions via UI to trigger NextBestDirectionSection display
context_for_next_testing_agent: NextBestDirectionSection renders when user has history. The recommendation algorithm analyzes history value_tags (contribution, recovery, order, harm, avoidance) to determine the 'next best direction'. The avoidance/harm pattern detection requires >40% negative value_tags in recent 10 decisions, which depends on backend NLP classification. Collective gap comparison fetches data via fetchCollectiveLayer from dataService.js.

frontend_tests.test: NextBestDirectionSection renders when user has history
frontend_tests.details: Component appears after first decision is added
Shows appropriate reason like 'מבוסס על ניתוח איזון כיוונים'

recommendation_logic.priority_1: Negative drift (harm/avoidance >40%) → recommends recovery or order
recommendation_logic.priority_2: Collective gap (user below collective >15%) → recommends gap direction
recommendation_logic.priority_3: Replay blind spots → recommends unexplored direction
recommendation_logic.priority_5: Replay preferences → recommends frequently replayed direction
recommendation_logic.priority_6: Balance deficit → recommends lowest positive score direction

ui_elements.compass_svg: Animated direction indicator with strength arc
ui_elements.insight_section: Explanation of why this direction is recommended

data_sources.collectiveData: Cross-user collective layer comparison via fetchCollectiveLayer

note_on_avoidance_pattern_testing: The avoidance pattern detection (recommending 'order' when avoidanceCount > harmCount) requires backend to classify decisions with 'avoidance' value_tag. During testing, decisions were classified as 'recovery' by backend NLP, so the negative drift threshold wasn't triggered. The frontend logic is implemented correctly - it depends on backend classification results.
